summary: Well-structured, small, single-responsibility methods with clear contracts and proper async patterns lead to maintainable, testable VB.NET code compatible with both MVC and MVVM architectures.
reasoning: Single-responsibility methods are easier to unit test in isolation, which is critical for MVC controllers and MVVM view models.
Async/Await prevents thread starvation in ASP.NET MVC controllers and keeps WPF/WinForms MVVM UIs responsive.
Guard clauses at method entry fail fast with clear error messages, reducing debugging time.
Separating concerns across layers (View, Controller/ViewModel, Model, Service) ensures each method has a clear role.
references: Async Programming with Async and Await (VB): https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/visual-basic/programming-guide/concepts/async/
Clean Code by Robert C. Martin (method design principles)
